verses.esv: The oracle that Habakkuk the prophet saw.
verses.kjv: THE burden which Habakkuk the prophet did see.
verses.bsb: This is the burden that Habakkuk the prophet received in a vision:

verses.esv: O LORD, how long shall I cry for help, and you will not hear? Or cry to you “Violence!” and you will not save?
verses.kjv: O Lord, how long shall I cry, and thou wilt not hear! even cry out unto thee of violence, and thou wilt not save!
verses.bsb: How long, O LORD, must I call for help but You do not hear, or cry out to You, “Violence!” but You do not save?

verses.esv: Why do you make me see iniquity, and why do you idly look at wrong? Destruction and violence are before me; strife and contention arise.
verses.kjv: Why dost thou shew me iniquity, and cause me to behold grievance? for spoiling and violence are before me: and there are that raise up strife and contention.
verses.bsb: Why do You make me see iniquity? Why do You tolerate wrongdoing? Destruction and violence are before me. Strife is ongoing, and conflict abounds.

verses.esv: So the law is paralyzed, and justice never goes forth. For the wicked surround the righteous; so justice goes forth perverted.
verses.kjv: Therefore the law is slacked, and judgment doth never go forth: for the wicked doth compass about the righteous; therefore wrong judgment proceedeth.
verses.bsb: Therefore the law is paralyzed, and justice never goes forth. For the wicked hem in the righteous, so that justice is perverted.

verses.esv: “Look among the nations, and see; wonder and be astounded. For I am doing a work in your days that you would not believe if told.
verses.kjv: ¶ Behold ye among the heathen, and regard, and wonder marvellously: for I will work a work in your days, which ye will not believe, though it be told you.
verses.bsb: “Look at the nations and observe— be utterly astounded! For I am doing a work in your days that you would never believe even if someone told you.

verses.esv: For behold, I am raising up the Chaldeans, that bitter and hasty nation, who march through the breadth of the earth, to seize dwellings not their own.
verses.kjv: For, lo, I raise up the Chaldeans, that bitter and hasty nation, which shall march through the breadth of the land, to possess the dwellingplaces that are not theirs.
verses.bsb: For behold, I am raising up the Chaldeans— that ruthless and impetuous nation which marches through the breadth of the earth to seize dwellings not their own.

verses.esv: They are dreaded and fearsome; their justice and dignity go forth from themselves.
verses.kjv: They are terrible and dreadful: their judgment and their dignity shall proceed of themselves.
verses.bsb: They are dreaded and feared; from themselves they derive justice and sovereignty.

verses.esv: Their horses are swifter than leopards, more fierce than the evening wolves; their horsemen press proudly on. Their horsemen come from afar; they fly like an eagle swift to devour.
verses.kjv: Their horses also are swifter than the leopards, and are more fierce than the evening wolves: and their horsemen shall spread themselves, and their horsemen shall come from far; they shall fly as the eagle that hasteth to eat.
verses.bsb: Their horses are swifter than leopards, fiercer than wolves of the night. Their horsemen charge ahead, and their cavalry comes from afar. They fly like a vulture, swooping down to devour.

verses.esv: They all come for violence, all their faces forward. They gather captives like sand.
verses.kjv: They shall come all for violence: their faces shall sup up as the east wind, and they shall gather the captivity as the sand.
verses.bsb: All of them come bent on violence; their hordes advance like the east wind; they gather prisoners like sand.

verses.esv: At kings they scoff, and at rulers they laugh. They laugh at every fortress, for they pile up earth and take it.
verses.kjv: And they shall scoff at the kings, and the princes shall be a scorn unto them: they shall deride every strong hold; for they shall heap dust, and take it.
verses.bsb: They scoff at kings and make rulers an object of scorn. They laugh at every fortress and build up siege ramps to seize it.

verses.esv: Then they sweep by like the wind and go on, guilty men, whose own might is their god!”
verses.kjv: Then shall his mind change, and he shall pass over, and offend, imputing this his power unto his god.
verses.bsb: Then they sweep by like the wind and pass on through. They are guilty; their own strength is their god.”

verses.esv: Are you not from everlasting, O LORD my God, my Holy One? We shall not die. O LORD, you have ordained them as a judgment, and you, O Rock, have established them for reproof.
verses.kjv: ¶ Art thou not from everlasting, O Lord my God, mine Holy One? we shall not die. O Lord, thou hast ordained them for judgment; and, O mighty God, thou hast established them for correction.
verses.bsb: Are You not from everlasting, O LORD, my God, my Holy One? We will not die. O LORD, You have appointed them to execute judgment; O Rock, You have established them for correction.

verses.esv: You who are of purer eyes than to see evil and cannot look at wrong, why do you idly look at traitors and remain silent when the wicked swallows up the man more righteous than he?
verses.kjv: Thou art of purer eyes than to behold evil, and canst not look on iniquity: wherefore lookest thou upon them that deal treacherously, and holdest thy tongue when the wicked devoureth the man that is more righteous than he?
verses.bsb: Your eyes are too pure to look upon evil, and You cannot tolerate wrongdoing. So why do You tolerate the faithless? Why are You silent while the wicked swallow up those more righteous than themselves?

verses.esv: You make mankind like the fish of the sea, like crawling things that have no ruler.
verses.kjv: And makest men as the fishes of the sea, as the creeping things, that have no ruler over them?
verses.bsb: You have made men like the fish of the sea, like creeping things that have no ruler.

verses.esv: He brings all of them up with a hook; he drags them out with his net; he gathers them in his dragnet; so he rejoices and is glad.
verses.kjv: They take up all of them with the angle, they catch them in their net, and gather them in their drag: therefore they rejoice and are glad.
verses.bsb: The foe pulls all of them up with a hook; he catches them in his dragnet, and gathers them in his fishing net; so he rejoices gladly.

verses.esv: Therefore he sacrifices to his net and makes offerings to his dragnet; for by them he lives in luxury, and his food is rich.
verses.kjv: Therefore they sacrifice unto their net, and burn incense unto their drag; because by them their portion is fat, and their meat plenteous.
verses.bsb: Therefore he sacrifices to his dragnet and burns incense to his fishing net, for by these things his portion is sumptuous and his food is rich.

verses.esv: Is he then to keep on emptying his net and mercilessly killing nations forever? I will take my stand at my watchpost and station myself on the tower, and look out to see what he will say to me, and what I will answer concerning my complaint.
verses.kjv: Shall they therefore empty their net, and not spare continually to slay the nations?
verses.bsb: Will he, therefore, empty his net and continue to slay nations without mercy?

verses.esv: And the LORD answered me: “Write the vision; make it plain on tablets, so he may run who reads it.
verses.kjv: And the Lord answered me, and said, Write the vision, and make it plain upon tables, that he may run that readeth it.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD answered me:  “Write down this vision and clearly inscribe it on tablets, so that a herald may run with it.

verses.esv: For still the vision awaits its appointed time; it hastens to the end—it will not lie. If it seems slow, wait for it; it will surely come; it will not delay.
verses.kjv: For the vision is yet for an appointed time, but at the end it shall speak, and not lie: though it tarry, wait for it; because it will surely come, it will not tarry.
verses.bsb: For the vision awaits an appointed time; it testifies of the end and does not lie. Though it lingers, wait for it, since it will surely come and will not delay.

verses.esv: “Behold, his soul is puffed up; it is not upright within him, but the righteous shall live by his faith.
verses.kjv: Behold, his soul which is lifted up is not upright in him: but the just shall live by his faith.
verses.bsb: Look at the proud one; his soul is not upright— but the righteous will live by faith—

verses.esv: “Moreover, wine is a traitor, an arrogant man who is never at rest. His greed is as wide as Sheol; like death he has never enough. He gathers for himself all nations and collects as his own all peoples.”
verses.kjv: ¶ Yea also, because he transgresseth by wine, he is a proud man, neither keepeth at home, who enlargeth his desire as hell, and is as death, and cannot be satisfied, but gathereth unto him all nations, and heapeth unto him all people:
verses.bsb: and wealth indeed betrays him. He is an arrogant man never at rest. He enlarges his appetite like Sheol, and like Death, he is never satisfied. He gathers all the nations to himself and collects all the peoples as his own.

verses.esv: Shall not all these take up their taunt against him, with scoffing and riddles for him, and say, “Woe to him who heaps up what is not his own— for how long?— and loads himself with pledges!”
verses.kjv: Shall not all these take up a parable against him, and a taunting proverb against him, and say, Woe to him that increaseth that which is not his! how long? and to him that ladeth himself with thick clay!
verses.bsb: Will not all of these take up a taunt against him, speaking with mockery and derision:   ‘Woe to him who amasses what is not his and makes himself rich with many loans! How long will this go on?’

verses.esv: Will not your debtors suddenly arise, and those awake who will make you tremble? Then you will be spoil for them.
verses.kjv: Shall they not rise up suddenly that shall bite thee, and awake that shall vex thee, and thou shalt be for booties unto them?
verses.bsb: Will not your creditors suddenly arise and those who disturb you awaken? Then you will become their prey.

verses.esv: Because you have plundered many nations, all the remnant of the peoples shall plunder you, for the blood of man and violence to the earth, to cities and all who dwell in them.
verses.kjv: Because thou hast spoiled many nations, all the remnant of the people shall spoil thee; because of men’s blood, and for the violence of the land, of the city, and of all that dwell therein.
verses.bsb: Because you have plundered many nations, the remnant of the people will plunder you— because of your bloodshed against man and your violence against the land, the city, and all their dwellers.

verses.esv: “Woe to him who gets evil gain for his house, to set his nest on high, to be safe from the reach of harm!
verses.kjv: ¶ Woe to him that coveteth an evil covetousness to his house, that he may set his nest on high, that he may be delivered from the power of evil!
verses.bsb: Woe to him who builds his house by unjust gain, to place his nest on high and escape the hand of disaster!

verses.esv: You have devised shame for your house by cutting off many peoples; you have forfeited your life.
verses.kjv: Thou hast consulted shame to thy house by cutting off many people, and hast sinned against thy soul.
verses.bsb: You have plotted shame for your house by cutting off many peoples and forfeiting your life.

verses.esv: For the stone will cry out from the wall, and the beam from the woodwork respond.
verses.kjv: For the stone shall cry out of the wall, and the beam out of the timber shall answer it.
verses.bsb: For the stones will cry out from the wall, and the rafters will echo it from the woodwork.

verses.esv: “Woe to him who builds a town with blood and founds a city on iniquity!
verses.kjv: ¶ Woe to him that buildeth a town with blood, and stablisheth a city by iniquity!
verses.bsb: Woe to him who builds a city with bloodshed and establishes a town by iniquity!

verses.esv: Behold, is it not from the LORD of hosts that peoples labor merely for fire, and nations weary themselves for nothing?
verses.kjv: Behold, is it not of the Lord of hosts that the people shall labour in the very fire, and the people shall weary themselves for very vanity?
verses.bsb: Is it not indeed from the LORD of Hosts that the labor of the people only feeds the fire, and the nations weary themselves in vain?

verses.esv: For the earth will be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the LORD as the waters cover the sea.
verses.kjv: For the earth shall be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the Lord, as the waters cover the sea.
verses.bsb: For the earth will be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the LORD as the waters cover the sea.

verses.esv: “Woe to him who makes his neighbors drink— you pour out your wrath and make them drunk, in order to gaze at their nakedness!
verses.kjv: ¶ Woe unto him that giveth his neighbour drink, that puttest thy bottle to him, and makest him drunken also, that thou mayest look on their nakedness!
verses.bsb: Woe to him who gives drink to his neighbors, pouring it from the wineskin until they are drunk, in order to gaze at their nakedness!

verses.esv: You will have your fill of shame instead of glory. Drink, yourself, and show your uncircumcision! The cup in the LORD's right hand will come around to you, and utter shame will come upon your glory!
verses.kjv: Thou art filled with shame for glory: drink thou also, and let thy foreskin be uncovered: the cup of the Lord’s right hand shall be turned unto thee, and shameful spewing shall be on thy glory.
verses.bsb: You will be filled with shame instead of glory. You too must drink and expose your uncircumcision! The cup in the LORD’s right hand will come around to you, and utter disgrace will cover your glory.

verses.esv: The violence done to Lebanon will overwhelm you, as will the destruction of the beasts that terrified them, for the blood of man and violence to the earth, to cities and all who dwell in them.
verses.kjv: For the violence of Lebanon shall cover thee, and the spoil of beasts, which made them afraid, because of men’s blood, and for the violence of the land, of the city, and of all that dwell therein.
verses.bsb: For your violence against Lebanon will overwhelm you, and the destruction of animals will terrify you, because of your bloodshed against men and your violence against the land, the city, and all their dwellers.

verses.esv: “What profit is an idol when its maker has shaped it, a metal image, a teacher of lies? For its maker trusts in his own creation when he makes speechless idols!
verses.kjv: ¶ What profiteth the graven image that the maker thereof hath graven it; the molten image, and a teacher of lies, that the maker of his work trusteth therein, to make dumb idols?
verses.bsb: What use is an idol, that a craftsman should carve it— or an image, a teacher of lies? For its maker trusts in his own creation; he makes idols that cannot speak.

verses.esv: Woe to him who says to a wooden thing, Awake; to a silent stone, Arise! Can this teach? Behold, it is overlaid with gold and silver, and there is no breath at all in it.
verses.kjv: Woe unto him that saith to the wood, Awake; to the dumb stone, Arise, it shall teach! Behold, it is laid over with gold and silver, and there is no breath at all in the midst of it.
verses.bsb: Woe to him who says to wood, ‘Awake!’ or to silent stone, ‘Arise!’ Can it give guidance? Behold, it is overlaid with gold and silver, yet there is no breath in it at all.”

verses.esv: But the LORD is in his holy temple; let all the earth keep silence before him.”
verses.kjv: But the Lord is in his holy temple: let all the earth keep silence before him.
verses.bsb: But the LORD is in His holy temple; let all the earth be silent before Him.

verses.esv: A prayer of Habakkuk the prophet, according to Shigionoth.
verses.kjv: A PRAYER of Habakkuk the prophet upon Shigionoth.
verses.bsb: This is a prayer of Habakkuk the prophet, according to Shigionoth:

verses.esv: O LORD, I have heard the report of you, and your work, O LORD, do I fear. In the midst of the years revive it; in the midst of the years make it known; in wrath remember mercy.
verses.kjv: O Lord, I have heard thy speech, and was afraid: O Lord, revive thy work in the midst of the years, in the midst of the years make known; in wrath remember mercy.
verses.bsb: O LORD, I have heard the report of You; I stand in awe, O LORD, of Your deeds. Revive them in these years; make them known in these years. In Your wrath, remember mercy!

verses.esv: God came from Teman, and the Holy One from Mount Paran. Selah His splendor covered the heavens, and the earth was full of his praise.
verses.kjv: God came from Teman, and the Holy One from mount Paran. Selah. His glory covered the heavens, and the earth was full of his praise.
verses.bsb: God came from Teman, and the Holy One from Mount Paran. Selah His glory covered the heavens, and His praise filled the earth.

verses.esv: His brightness was like the light; rays flashed from his hand; and there he veiled his power.
verses.kjv: And his brightness was as the light; he had horns coming out of his hand: and there was the hiding of his power.
verses.bsb: His radiance was like the sunlight; rays flashed from His hand, where His power is hidden.

verses.esv: Before him went pestilence, and plague followed at his heels.
verses.kjv: Before him went the pestilence, and burning coals went forth at his feet.
verses.bsb: Plague went before Him, and fever followed in His steps.

verses.esv: He stood and measured the earth; he looked and shook the nations; then the eternal mountains were scattered; the everlasting hills sank low. His were the everlasting ways.
verses.kjv: He stood, and measured the earth: he beheld, and drove asunder the nations; and the everlasting mountains were scattered, the perpetual hills did bow: his ways are everlasting.
verses.bsb: He stood and measured the earth; He looked and startled the nations; the ancient mountains crumbled; the perpetual hills collapsed. His ways are everlasting.

verses.esv: I saw the tents of Cushan in affliction; the curtains of the land of Midian did tremble.
verses.kjv: I saw the tents of Cushan in affliction: and the curtains of the land of Midian did tremble.
verses.bsb: I saw the tents of Cushan in distress; the curtains of Midian were trembling.

verses.esv: Was your wrath against the rivers, O LORD? Was your anger against the rivers, or your indignation against the sea, when you rode on your horses, on your chariot of salvation?
verses.kjv: Was the Lord displeased against the rivers? was thine anger against the rivers? was thy wrath against the sea, that thou didst ride upon thine horses and thy chariots of salvation?
verses.bsb: Were You angry at the rivers, O LORD? Was Your wrath against the streams? Did You rage against the sea when You rode on Your horses, on Your chariots of salvation?

verses.esv: You stripped the sheath from your bow, calling for many arrows. Selah You split the earth with rivers.
verses.kjv: Thy bow was made quite naked, according to the oaths of the tribes, even thy word. Selah. Thou didst cleave the earth with rivers.
verses.bsb: You brandished Your bow; You called for many arrows. Selah You split the earth with rivers.

verses.esv: The mountains saw you and writhed; the raging waters swept on; the deep gave forth its voice; it lifted its hands on high.
verses.kjv: The mountains saw thee, and they trembled: the overflowing of the water passed by: the deep uttered his voice, and lifted up his hands on high.
verses.bsb: The mountains saw You and quaked; torrents of water swept by. The deep roared with its voice and lifted its hands on high.

verses.esv: The sun and moon stood still in their place at the light of your arrows as they sped, at the flash of your glittering spear.
verses.kjv: The sun and moon stood still in their habitation: at the light of thine arrows they went, and at the shining of thy glittering spear.
verses.bsb: Sun and moon stood still in their places at the flash of Your flying arrows, at the brightness of Your shining spear.

verses.esv: You marched through the earth in fury; you threshed the nations in anger.
verses.kjv: Thou didst march through the land in indignation, thou didst thresh the heathen in anger.
verses.bsb: You marched across the earth with fury; You threshed the nations in wrath.

verses.esv: You went out for the salvation of your people, for the salvation of your anointed. You crushed the head of the house of the wicked, laying him bare from thigh to neck. Selah
verses.kjv: Thou wentest forth for the salvation of thy people, even for salvation with thine anointed; thou woundedst the head out of the house of the wicked, by discovering the foundation unto the neck. Selah.
verses.bsb: You went forth for the salvation of Your people, to save Your anointed. You crushed the head of the house of the wicked and stripped him from head to toe. Selah

verses.esv: You pierced with his own arrows the heads of his warriors, who came like a whirlwind to scatter me, rejoicing as if to devour the poor in secret.
verses.kjv: Thou didst strike through with his staves the head of his villages: they came out as a whirlwind to scatter me: their rejoicing was as to devour the poor secretly.
verses.bsb: With his own spear You pierced his head, when his warriors stormed out to scatter us, gloating as though ready to secretly devour the weak.

verses.esv: You trampled the sea with your horses, the surging of mighty waters.
verses.kjv: Thou didst walk through the sea with thine horses, through the heap of great waters.
verses.bsb: You trampled the sea with Your horses, churning the great waters.

verses.esv: I hear, and my body trembles; my lips quiver at the sound; rottenness enters into my bones; my legs tremble beneath me. Yet I will quietly wait for the day of trouble to come upon people who invade us.
verses.kjv: When I heard, my belly trembled; my lips quivered at the voice: rottenness entered into my bones, and I trembled in myself, that I might rest in the day of trouble: when he cometh up unto the people, he will invade them with his troops.
verses.bsb: I heard and trembled within; my lips quivered at the sound. Decay entered my bones; I trembled where I stood. Yet I must wait patiently for the day of distress to come upon the people who invade us.

verses.esv: Though the fig tree should not blossom, nor fruit be on the vines, the produce of the olive fail and the fields yield no food, the flock be cut off from the fold and there be no herd in the stalls,
verses.kjv: ¶ Although the fig tree shall not blossom, neither shall fruit be in the vines; the labour of the olive shall fail, and the fields shall yield no meat; the flock shall be cut off from the fold, and there shall be no herd in the stalls:
verses.bsb: Though the fig tree does not bud and no fruit is on the vines, though the olive crop fails and the fields produce no food, though the sheep are cut off from the fold and no cattle are in the stalls,

verses.esv: yet I will rejoice in the LORD; I will take joy in the God of my salvation.
verses.kjv: Yet I will rejoice in the Lord, I will joy in the God of my salvation.
verses.bsb: yet I will exult in the LORD; I will rejoice in the God of my salvation!

verses.esv: GOD, the Lord, is my strength; he makes my feet like the deer's; he makes me tread on my high places. To the choirmaster: with stringed instruments.
verses.kjv: The Lord God is my strength, and he will make my feet like hinds’ feet, and he will make me to walk upon mine high places. To the chief singer on my stringed instruments.
verses.bsb: GOD the Lord is my strength; He makes my feet like those of a deer; He makes me walk upon the heights!   For the choirmaster. With stringed instruments.
